title: carissa edulis var. revoluta
intro: Carissa edulis var. revoluta, also known as the "Night Jasmine" or "Wild Plum", is a shrub or small tree native to southern Africa, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and red or yellow fruits. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Carissa edulis var. revoluta include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the treatment of various infections, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and protect against cell damage. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, stems, and fruits are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
